title: [Calyx] Compiling structural Calyx to `hw`
body:
Currently, we can roundtrip Calyx programs between the [native][] infrastructure and the CIRCT dialect for Calyx. This means that we can, in essence, run Calyx optimizations in both the systems and then have the native compiler [lowers Calyx programs][lowering] into a group and control-free representation. This representation is intentionally designed to be very close to RTL programs and the native compiler transliterates assignments in Calyx to mutliplexed assignments in SystemVerilog.

We should have a similar lowering pass in the CIRCT infrastructure that can compile control-free Calyx programs into the `hw` dialect. This will unlock the optimizations performed by the `comb` and `seq` compilers and allow us to build a robust RTL backend for Calyx.

With this in hand, once the Calyx lowering passes are reimplemented in CIRCT (https://github.com/llvm/circt/issues/2375), we can have a standalone Calyx-CIRCT infrastructure that is independent of the native infrastructure. The [lowering][] pass in Calyx is a good starting point for reimplementing this in CIRCT.

Additionally, do people have opinions on how to use the `comb`, `seq`, and `hw` dialects for this? At a high-level, we need access to verilog's continuous assignment statements (`assign`) and ternary expressions. We can probably use the `comb` primitives to realize the guard expressions for the ternaries. We also need to use `module`s to talk about component instantiation in Calyx.

[native]: https://github.com/cucapra/calyx
[lowering]: https://github.com/cucapra/calyx/blob/master/src/backend/verilog.rs
